In most cities the street cleaning department is the first to receive the pruning knife, Money is required by the street cleaning department for two general purposes the proper equipment of the department and operating expenses. The work is performed to such an extent as to remove all but the fine dust and keeps a continuous condition of cleanliness at all times. headlines.title: The Care of Trees.
headlines.fullText:
attack trees and plants, and the remedies necessary to eradicate them. Many people seem to think that just so something is done to a tree It doesn't at: ter much what remedy la applied out with trees, as with human beings, sier ciflo diseases require amongwant dies, and just as you would want a doctor who understood his business If meg trees need the services of one having scientific knowledge of their diseases.
It would take a large volume to name and describe all the Insect peste and different varieties of trees and plants, and the very best authorities on this subject tell us there are still many things to be learned. Then if the list of insect pests and diseases in such a long one, does It not neem imperative that one who undertaken to care for trees and plants should have expert knowledge an to their needs? A tree represents " very Important vaitan since it renders a service which cannot possibly be had from any other Thousands of trees die each year simply from the lack of proper attention cost of from 11 to go a tree. If there is any method by which these trees can be saved and brought up to servecenble aire it is worth having. For- Jecable size ze avenue depart. ments to look after the parks, but there are a great many things that need to he done for trees besides merely keeping down the weeds and cutting off the broken branches. Too often there is no one connected with the park department who knows how to prune a tree properly, for there is a right way to de this which Is often ignored, Many times the park department does not understand the troubles that exist in the parks they control, hence many valuable trees are destroyed for want of every Every city with a population of 30,- is amply gunlined to look after the trees, and to combat the troubles that may arise among them. He could also to plant, how they should be pianted. and how they should be pruned cared for: all trees in the parks and on the streets should be under his care and supervision. If any one may think this an unnecessary expense, he may learn from either the state, or the United States department of agriculture that more than 50 per cent of the trees in Texas are Infested.
This is too serious a matter to be passed by with nilght consideration, and It would be well for the people to take the matter in hand and ask the city ad- minden propor a properly trained tree,warden to ad- Vise the people how to care for their trees
If there were an efficient tree warden in Fort Worth, with the proper au- should be, there will change In the appearance of the city. We should see fewer dead and dying trees along our streets and upon our lawns and more green and flourishing trees which would be fulfilling their propre destiny in furnishing the abundant shade and beauty for which they were created. It is only through the services of an efficient tree warden that we may hope to have healthy. uniform frees along the streets and drive- mbanute. wayR. D. J. TACKETT.

headlines.title: STRAWBERRIES OUT
headlines.subtitle: San Angelo Season Several Weeks Ahead of Time.
headlines.fullText:
BAN ANGELO, Texas, Jan. 31. Strawberries are now being picked and the market in San Angelo, This abend of previous seasons by tere weeks.

headlines.title: LIFESAVERS WILL TAKE EXAMINATIONS
headlines.subtitle: Tests Will Decide Successor to Capt. Hutchings of Galveston, Who Died Recently.
headlines.fullText:
GALVESTON, Jan. 31 .- Keepers of the eight life saving stations composing the Ninth district, of which Galveston Is the headquarters, will participate in a competitive examination to fill the position of superintendent, made vacant by the death recently of Captain W. A. Hutchings.
The examination will be held under the civil service rules January 28.
